narrative: Jindal Stainless is the overall leader for the GHG and Scope 3 theme in FY2022-23 on reported Scope 1, Scope 2 and physical-output intensity, with 2.58 MtCO2e Scope 1, 0.74 MtCO2e Scope 2 and 2.1 tCO2e/tcs. JSW leads only the Scope 3 absolute and Scope 3 turnover-intensity indicators, but this may reflect narrower Scope 3 coverage rather than inherently superior value-chain performance. Tata’s FY2022-23 numbers are on a consolidated basis, including international operations, so Tata’s 75.75 MtCO2e Scope 1 and 13.10 MtCO2e Scope 3 should not be treated as directly comparable with standalone Indian peers. SAIL is the laggard on physical-output intensity at 2.8 tCO2e/tcs and per-rupee Scope 1+2 intensity, reflecting integrated BF-BOF assets, legacy plant footprint and captive/process energy intensity. On a process basis, Jindal’s scrap/EAF stainless model structurally explains its strong performance, while Tata, JSW and SAIL remain dominated by coal-intensive primary steelmaking.
